rulings.Case Title: IN RE: COALITION OF UNIVERSITY EMPLOYEES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn And Ntc Of Mtn On Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 23, 2010, line 9. PETITIONER COALITION OF UNIVERSITY EMPLOYEES, INTERNATIONAL BROTHERHOOD OF TEAMSTERS'S Motion On Petition To Compel Arbitration is granted. The January 20, 2010 letter from the U.C. to C.U.E. constitutes a contract to arbitrate. The letter, followed by the actions of the U.C. in conformity with an intent to arbitrate provided a mutual assent. Consideration is shown by petitioner's implied promise to forebear filing a P.E.R.B. charge in exchange for the agreement to arbitrate. Furthermore, the promissory estoppel doctrine applies in that petitioner reasonably and detrimentally relied on respondent's promise to arbitrate. Because of that representation, and the efforts by both parties in furtherance of arbitration, petitioner lost its opportunity to file a P.E.R.B. charge. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ABE NEWMAN et al VS. SURESH PARMAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 23, 2010, line 11. PLAINTIFFS ABE NEWMAN, PHYLIS KURZBAD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Triable issue of material fact raised regarding whether the roof repair was a maintenance issue and defendant's responsibility, (lease paragraph 10) or whether the damage constituted a partial destruction requiring plaintiff to make the repairs and provide a rent reduction (lease paragraph 21). Summary adjudication granted, no triable issue of fact, as to constructive eviction, discrimination, affirmative defense 3d (waiver), and 3e (retaliation). Summary adjudication denied as to affirmative defenses 3J(1) (commercial frustration) and 3b (repair and deduct) due to conflict in lease provisions 10 and 21. Supplemental brief filed September 20, 2010 not considered as untimely. =(302/CWW)
